subject: Statistics
title: Pandemic Forecasting: Exponential Surprises
description: A quick win: understand Pandemic Forecasting: Exponential Surprises.

hook.text: In January 2020, COVID-19 had 800 confirmed cases. By April, it was 3 million. Humans can't intuit exponential growth.

buildup.text: Epidemic models use the reproduction number R₀ to forecast spread. R₀ > 1 means exponential growth.

discovery.text: Early COVID models varied wildly because small differences in R₀ produce enormous differences in outcomes.

twist.text: Models that assumed constant behavior failed. People changed behavior, policies shifted — the system adapted.

climax.text: Pandemic forecasting showed both the power and limits of models. The uncertainty was the most honest signal.

punchline.text: Exponential growth breaks intuition. The models were imperfect but essential.

quiz.question: Why did early COVID-19 models produce wildly different forecasts?
options: Small differences in the reproduction number R₀ create enormous differences in exponential outcomes
All models used the same assumptions
